what_it_does: This file is a self-contained acceptance test script for the S-R9.1 CI scaffolder. It imports the scaffolder functions and verifies cadence-to-cron conversion, GitHub workflow generation, GitLab job generation, scaffold_from_routine target paths and scheduling, the 'generates-not-installs' off-machine note, and fail-loud behavior for an unknown provider. It records results in PASS/FAIL lists, prints them, and exits with code 1 if any check fails.
observations: Uses a hand-rolled check(n, c, d='') helper instead of pytest/unittest.
Tests _cadence_to_cron for OnCalendar daily, every:N seconds, and unmappable cadences.
Verifies GitHub workflow contains anthopics/claude-code-action@v1, secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, the prompt, and a cron schedule.
Checks that a missing cron falls back to an issue_comment on-demand trigger.
Verifies GitLab job contains claude -p, mcp__gitlab, and --model opus.
Uses routine_registry()['self_status'] to test scaffold_from_routine for GitHub.
Checks GitLab scaffold target path is .gitlab-ci.yml.
Checks the generated scaffold carries a note emphasizing off-machine / not-the-Company generation.
Expects a ValueError with 'unknown provider' for an unsupported provider.
Inserts the project root into sys.path so runtime modules can be imported.
standouts: The file doubles as both acceptance spec and executable runner with PASS/FAIL reporting.
It explicitly tests the 'generates-not-installs' / off-machine principle as a behavioral requirement.
It validates string-level contents of generated CI artifacts rather than structural parsing.
conventions: Docstring at the top states scope, constraints, and the 'pure generation — no network, no install' rule.
Simple print-based test reporting with global PASS/FAIL accumulators.
Imports private helper _cadence_to_cron directly for unit-style verification.
concerns: String-substring checks on generated templates are brittle if template wording changes.
No cleanup or isolation of sys.path mutation.
Relies on hardcoded routine names and model strings from the registry.
Global PASS/FAIL lists could be accidentally reused if the module is imported.
notes: A runnable acceptance harness that checks the CI scaffolder produces correct GitHub/GitLab artifacts, maps cadences to cron, falls back to on-demand, and rejects unknown providers.
questions: What is the full set of cadence formats _cadence_to_cron is expected to support?
What does the self_status routine's cadence actually look like, and is it stable?
How are the generated workflow files intended to be committed or validated in real repositories?
Why is the GitLab job tested only for model=opus and not for scheduling or target-path details?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a pure-generation, no-network acceptance test for the CI scaffolder.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to provide a lightweight, self-running acceptance gate that documents the expected behavior of the CI scaffolder and catches regressions in cron mapping, provider output, and error handling.
novelty: It encodes acceptance criteria as a plain script with a custom pass/fail harness rather than using a standard test framework, and it explicitly tests a 'generates-not-installs' policy note.
contribution: A runnable checklist of expected scaffolder outputs and error behavior that can serve as a regression guard and as living documentation of the CI scaffold contract.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test script for the S-R9.1 CI scaffolder that verifies cadence-to-cron conversion, GitHub and GitLab workflow generation, target paths, off-machine notes, and fail-loud handling of unknown providers.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a self-contained, runnable acceptance gate for the CI scaffold generator without network or installation side effects.

symbols.does: Manual pass/fail assertion helper used to record and print acceptance check results.
symbols.description: Takes a test name n, a boolean condition c, and an optional detail string d. It appends n to PASS or FAIL depending on c, prints a PASS or FAIL line (showing detail only when the check fails), and is used throughout the script to accumulate acceptance results before the final summary and exit code decision.
